Letter of Intent
Non-Binding — ESOP Acquisition

{{sellerName}}
Company
{{companyName}}
Date
{{sentDate}}
Reference
{{ref}}
§ 01Introduction

Dear {{sellerName}},

Forhemit Stewardship Management Co. ("Forhemit") is pleased to present this Letter of Intent ("LOI") outlining the proposed terms for the acquisition of 100% of the equity interests in {{companyName}} (the "Company") by an Employee Stock Ownership Plan ("ESOP") to be established by the Company for the benefit of its employees.

This LOI is intended to serve as a basis for further negotiation and due diligence. Except as expressly stated in the Binding Provisions section below, this LOI is non-binding and does not create any legal obligation on either party.

§ 02Transaction Structure
Purchase & Consideration
{{purchasePrice}}
Independent Fair Market Value appraisal per IRC §401(a)(28)
ESOP Trust — new plan sponsored by the Company
100% of outstanding equity interests
Consideration Mix
{{day1Cash}} (SBA 7(a) loan proceeds)
{{sellerNoteAmount}} at {{sellerNoteRate}}, {{sellerNoteTerm}} term
Entity Requirements
{{entityType}}
{{conversionRequired}}
§ 03Financing

The transaction will be financed through a combination of SBA 7(a) loan proceeds and a subordinated seller note:

{{sbaLenderName}} (to be confirmed during underwriting)
{{sbaLoanAmount}}
{{sbaLoanRate}} (estimated; subject to lender terms)
{{sbaLoanTerm}}
Note: SBA financing is subject to lender underwriting and SBA approval. Forhemit will coordinate lender outreach and submission, but the Seller acknowledges that financing is not guaranteed until the SBA issues a commitment letter.
§ 04Timeline

The target transaction timeline is 120 days from LOI execution, structured around four gates:

1.Gate 1 — Capital Committed (Day 30). SBA lender engagement confirmed, trustee and ERISA counsel retained, appraisal firm engaged.
2.Gate 2 — Valuation Confirmed (Day 60). QofE and FMV appraisal completed and accepted by trustee. Purchase price confirmed or adjusted.
3.Gate 3 — Operations Cleared (Day 90). Entity conversion complete, ESOP plan document drafted, SBA loan approved, all due diligence items cleared.
4.Gate 4 — Closing (Day 120). All documents executed, funds wired, ESOP trust holds 100% of equity.
{{targetClosingDate}}
§ 05Deposit
$15,000 (previously submitted)
Credited toward Engagement Retainer upon LOI execution
{{exclusivityDeposit}}
Refundable if transaction fails due to Gate failure not caused by Seller; non-refundable if Seller withdraws or breaches exclusivity
Escrow: All deposits are held in a separate escrow account at {{escrowBank}} and are not commingled with Forhemit operating funds. Detailed escrow terms will be provided upon execution.
§ 06Seller Representations

The Seller represents and warrants to Forhemit and the ESOP Trust, to the best of the Seller's knowledge, that:

A.The Seller has full authority to enter into this transaction and sell 100% of the equity interests in the Company.
B.The Company is current on all federal, state, and local tax filings and has no known material tax liabilities outside the ordinary course of business.
C.There are no pending or threatened legal proceedings, regulatory actions, or material disputes that could affect the transaction.
D.All financial information provided to Forhemit and its advisors is accurate and complete in all material respects.
E.The Company has not entered into any agreement that would prevent or materially impair the transaction, including change-of-control provisions or rights of first refusal.
§ 07Seller Obligations

During the period between LOI execution and closing, the Seller agrees to:

A.Operate in the ordinary course. Continue operating the Company in the ordinary course of business and not take any action that would materially impair the Company's value or the transaction.
B.Provide access. Provide Forhemit, the trustee, appraiser, and lender with reasonable access to the Company's books, records, employees, and facilities for due diligence purposes.
C.Cooperate on conversion. If applicable, cooperate with the entity conversion process, including executing required filings and tax elections.
D.Maintain confidentiality. Keep the terms of this LOI and the transaction confidential, except as required by law or as disclosed to the Seller's personal attorney and CPA.
§ 08Post-Close Transition
{{postCloseRole}}
{{transitionPeriod}}
{{governanceTerms}}

The specific terms of the Seller's post-close employment or consulting arrangement will be negotiated separately and documented in a definitive agreement.

§ 09Advisors & Costs
Forhemit Stewardship Management Co.
{{forhemitFee}} (per Engagement Letter)
{{sellerAttorney}} (at Seller's expense)
{{sellerCPA}}
Estimated {{transactionCosts}} — itemized in Transaction Cost Disclosure

All transaction costs, including legal, appraisal, and lender fees, are paid from the transaction proceeds at closing unless otherwise specified.

§ 10Tax Treatment

The transaction is structured to allow the Seller to elect tax deferral under IRC §1042, provided the following conditions are met:

A.The ESOP acquires at least 30% of the Company's equity (this LOI contemplates 100%).
B.The Seller reinvests the sale proceeds in Qualified Replacement Securities ("QRS") within the statutory period.
C.The Seller has held the equity for at least 3 years prior to the sale.
Not tax advice: Forhemit does not provide tax advice. The Seller should consult with a qualified tax advisor regarding §1042 eligibility, state tax implications, and the mechanics of the QRS reinvestment strategy.
§ 11Binding Provisions

Notwithstanding the non-binding nature of this LOI, the following provisions shall be legally binding upon execution by both parties:

A.Exclusivity. From the date of execution through {{exclusivityPeriod}}, the Seller shall not directly or indirectly solicit, encourage, negotiate, or enter into any agreement with any other party for the sale, merger, or recapitalization of the Company. The Seller shall promptly notify Forhemit of any unsolicited inquiries.
B.Confidentiality. The terms of this LOI and all information exchanged in connection with the transaction are confidential and subject to the NDA previously executed between the parties. Neither party shall disclose the terms of this LOI to any third party except as required by law or to their professional advisors who are bound by confidentiality obligations.
C.Deposit. The treatment and refundability of deposits as described in §05 is binding.
D.Governing law. This LOI shall be governed by the laws of the State of {{governingState}}.
E.Termination. Either party may terminate this LOI upon 10 business days' written notice if the transaction has not closed within {{maxTimeline}} of execution, or immediately upon mutual written agreement.
§ 12Contact

For questions about this Letter of Intent, please contact:

deals@forhemit.com
{{ref}}
—Seller Acceptance

By signing below, the Seller acknowledges receipt and review of this Letter of Intent and agrees to the terms set forth herein, including the binding provisions in §11.
